events.subtitle: Lent
events.title: The Liturgy of the Presanctified Gifts
events.description: The Liturgy of the Presanctified Gifts is an ancient vespers service celebrated on Wednesday and Friday evenings throughout Great Lent. No new offering is consecrated during it. The faithful receive Holy Communion from gifts consecrated at the previous Sunday's Liturgy, set aside and kept on the altar throughout the week. The service is among the most penitential of the entire liturgical year, marked by prostrations, dimmed lights, and the slow, low melody of the Lenten hymn "Let my prayer arise." It is a service of longing, a gathering in the growing dark of evening, fasting and waiting for the light of Pascha still weeks away. All are welcome to attend. Those who wish to receive Communion should fast from midnight and speak with Fr. Solomon beforehand.
